summary: Drew DeVault's December 2021 status update covers progress on his programming language (Hare), SourceHut, and visurf. The language saw cryptography improvements including AES cipher modes, AES-NI, HMAC, and blake2b, plus better C library linking. He started building a tetromino game using SDL2 bindings to test game development in the language. SourceHut work focused on todo.sr.ht's GraphQL API, which proved more complex than expected. He notes taking it easier heading into the holidays.
key_points: Hare's cryptography support expanded with AES cipher modes, AES-NI, HMAC, and blake2b implementations
Improved C library linking enabled third-party bindings like libui and SDL2 to emerge
Reflection support was removed from the language, signaling a design philosophy favoring simplicity
SourceHut's todo.sr.ht GraphQL API migration proved to be significantly more work than anticipated
visurf received community contributions including tab rearranging and navigation improvements
money_quotes: I am developing this to flesh out the SDL wrapper and get a feel for game development in the new language, but I also intend to take it on as a serious project to make a game which is fun to play.
Also, the reflection support introduced a few months ago has been removed.
This one ended up being a lot of work.
key_insight: Hare's development is maturing through real-world use cases like game development and cryptography, while deliberate removals like reflection show a commitment to language simplicity.
